title: Ensure only trusted container images are used

input_row.title: Ensure only trusted container images are used
input_row.description: Use Binary Authorization to allowlist (whitelist) only approved container registries.
input_row.rationale: Allowing unrestricted access to external container registries provides the opportunity for malicious or unapproved containers to be deployed into the cluster. Ensuring only trusted container images are used reduces this risk.
input_row.audit: Using Google Cloud Console: To check that Binary Authorization is enabled for the GKE cluster: 1. Go to the Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list 2. Select the cluster for which Binary Authorization is disabled. 3. Under the details pane, within the Security section, ensure that 'Binary Authorization' is set to 'Enabled'. Then, assess the contents of the policy: 4. Go to Binary Authorization by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/security/binary-authorization 5. Ensure a policy is defined and the project default rule is not configured to 'Allow all images'. Using Command Line: Check that Binary Authorization is enabled for the GKE cluster, first define 2 variables for Cluster Name and Zone, and then run the command below: gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE -- format json | jq .binaryAuthorization This will return one of 2 possible responses if Binary Authorization is enabled: Internal Only - General { "evaluationMode": "PROJECT_SINGLETON_POLICY_ENFORCE" } OR { "evaluationMode": "PROJECT_SINGLETON_POLICY_AUDIT_AND_WARN" } Then, assess the contents of the policy: gcloud container binauthz policy export > current-policy.yaml Ensure that the current policy is not configured to allow all images (evaluationMode: ALWAYS_ALLOW): cat current-policy.yaml ... defaultAdmissionRule: evaluationMode: ALWAYS_ALLOW
input_row.remediation: Using Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Binary Authorization by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/security/binary-authorization. 2. Enable the Binary Authorization API (if disabled). 3. Create an appropriate policy for use with the cluster. See https://cloud.google.com/binary-authorization/docs/policy-yaml-reference for guidance. 4. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list. 5. Select the cluster for which Binary Authorization is disabled. 6. Under the details pane, within the Security section, click on the pencil icon named Edit Binary Authorization. 7. Check the box next to Enable Binary Authorization. 8. Choose Enforce policy and provide a directory for the policy to be used. 9. Click SAVE CHANGES. Using Command Line: Update the cluster to enable Binary Authorization: Internal Only - General gcloud container cluster update --zone -- binauthz-evaluation-mode= Example: gcloud container clusters update $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE -- binauthz-evaluation-mode=PROJECT_SINGLETON_POLICY_ENFORCE See: https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/container/clusters/update#-- binauthz-evaluation-mode for more details around the evaluation modes available. Create a Binary Authorization Policy using the Binary Authorization Policy Reference: https://cloud.google.com/binary-authorization/docs/policy-yaml-reference for guidance. Import the policy file into Binary Authorization: gcloud container binauthz policy import
input_row.impact: All container images to be deployed to the cluster must be hosted within an approved container image registry. If public registries are not on the allowlist, a process for bringing commonly used container images into an approved private registry and keeping them up to date will be required.
input_row.default_value: By default, Binary Authorization is disabled along with container registry allowlisting.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- Can GCP APIs query this property? → Automated. The Binary Authorization settings can be queried via GCP APIs.
- Is this a standard GCP service property? → Automated. Binary Authorization is part of the GKE security features.
- Does Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK have methods to check this? → Automated. The `gcloud container clusters describe` command allows checking for Binary Authorization status.
- Does GCP CLI have commands to retrieve this? → Automated. The `gcloud container binauthz policy export` command can be used to retrieve the current policy.
- Does audit mention Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/GCP CLI commands? → Automated. The audit section provides gcloud commands explicitly.
- Is it about IAM, S3, EC2, VPC, CloudTrail, Config, KMS, etc.? → Automated. This pertains directly to GKE settings which are managed through GCP APIs.
- Is it about application code, OS configs, or visual review? → Manual. N/A, as this can be audited through API checks.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_binary_authorization_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. Use the command `gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE --format=json` to retrieve the Binary Authorization configuration of the GKE cluster.
   - Check the `binaryAuthorization.evaluationMode` to see if it is either `PROJECT_SINGLETON_POLICY_ENFORCE` or `PROJECT_SINGLETON_POLICY_AUDIT_AND_WARN`.
  
2. Retrieve the currently defined Binary Authorization policy using the command `gcloud container binauthz policy export`.
   - Ensure that the policy does not contain `defaultAdmissionRule.evaluationMode` set to `ALWAYS_ALLOW`.

**Pass/Fail Criteria:**
- The check passes if `binaryAuthorization.evaluationMode` is set to either `PROJECT_SINGLETON_POLICY_ENFORCE` or `PROJECT_SINGLETON_POLICY_AUDIT_AND_WARN`.
- The check passes if the output of `gcloud container binauthz policy export` shows that the `defaultAdmissionRule.evaluationMode` is NOT `ALWAYS_ALLOW`.

By utilizing these automated GCP API checks, organizations can effectively ensure compliance with the control to restrict GKE clusters to trusted container images.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively audited using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. Binary Authorization settings and policies can be queried and verified programmatically, making manual checks unnecessary for this control.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9
- The automation details are technically accurate. The use of `gcloud container clusters describe` to check the `binaryAuthorization.evaluationMode` and `gcloud container binauthz policy export` to verify the policy settings are valid methods.
- The program name follows a reasonable convention but could be more descriptive.
- The commands provided are correct and relevant to the control being audited.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name `gcp_gke_cluster_binary_authorization_enabled` could be improved for clarity. A suggestion would be `gcp_gke_binary_authorization_policy_compliance` to better reflect the focus on policy compliance.
- Consider adding a step to verify the specific trusted container registries that are allowed, as this is a critical part of ensuring only trusted images are used.
- It might be beneficial to include a check for any exceptions or overrides that might be present in the Binary Authorization policy, which could affect compliance.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded with accurate use of GCP capabilities and commands. The automation approach is appropriate for the control.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `gcp_gke_binary_authorization_policy_compliance` for better clarity and descriptiveness.
- Consider adding additional checks for allowed registries and potential policy exceptions to ensure comprehensive compliance verification.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**
Both AI opinions agree that the audit approach for this control can be automated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). The Binary Authorization settings and policies can be queried programmatically, making manual checks unnecessary. The commands provided, such as `gcloud container clusters describe` and `gcloud container binauthz policy export`, are appropriate for verifying the Binary Authorization status and policy compliance.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
gcp_gke_binary_authorization_policy_compliance

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
- API/CLI Method: Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) `container clusters describe`, `container binauthz policy export`
- Properties to Check: `binaryAuthorization.evaluationMode`, policy details from `binauthz policy export`
- Pass Criteria: `binaryAuthorization.evaluationMode` is set to `PROJECT_SINGLETON_POLICY_ENFORCE` or `PROJECT_SINGLETON_POLICY_AUDIT_AND_WARN`, and the policy allows only trusted registries.
- Fail Criteria: `binaryAuthorization.evaluationMode` is not set to the required values, or the policy allows untrusted registries.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**
There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported an automated approach, which aligns with the capabilities of GCP APIs.
